content.text:
I have enough for the map.

## Orientation: HashiCorp Nomad

**Project.** Nomad — a cluster scheduler/orchestrator (Go module `github.com/hashicorp/nomad`, go 1.26.4, `go.mod:1`). Repo root: `/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/nomad`. Note the confusing layout: the repo dir is itself named `nomad`, and the Go package you care about is `nomad/nomad/`.

**Top-level layout** (repo root):
- `nomad/` — **the server** (Raft, FSM, RPC endpoints, leadership, scheduling workers). This is where the rework lives.
- `client/` — the Nomad client agent (runs allocations on nodes).
- `command/agent/` — CLI + agent that constructs and owns either/both a server and a client; HTTP API.
- `scheduler/` — pure scheduling algorithms (bin-packing, feasibility), called by server workers.
- `helper/`, `lib/`, `plugins/`, `drivers/`, `api/`, `ui/`, `e2e/`, `testutil/` — support, task drivers, external Go client, Ember UI, tests.

**The object being reworked: `*nomad.Server`**
- Declared: `nomad/server.go:116` (`type Server struct`) — ~220 lines of fields: `config`, `logger`, `raft`/`raftLayer`/`raftStore`/`raftTransport`, `fsm`, `rpcListener`/`rpcServer`/`*rpcHandler`, `auth`, `serf`, `peersCache`, `connPool`, plus subsystem handles (`deploymentWatcher`, `volumeWatcher`, `nodeDrainer`, workers).
- Constructed: `NewServer(config, consulCatalog, consulConfigFunc)` at `nomad/server.go:336`.
- Lifecycle methods: `Shutdown()` `nomad/server.go:744`, `Reload(newConfig)` `nomad/server.go:910`.
- Wiring of subsystems (the sites that hand out narrowed handles): `setupConsulSyncer` `:1142`, `setupDeploymentWatcher` `:1156`, `setupVolumeWatcher` `:1178`, `setupNodeDrainer` `:1187`, `setupRPC` `:1203`, `setupWorkers` `:1930` / `setupWorkersLocked` `:1944`.
- Leadership transitions (where much re-wiring on restart would need to happen): `nomad/leader.go`.

**The pattern to audit.** Subsystems don't take `*Server`; they take a small locally-declared interface and *retain* it. Two flavors already visible:
1. **Shims** defined in package `nomad` that wrap the server and are passed out — `deploymentWatcherRaftShim{apply: s.raftApply}` (`nomad/deployment_watcher_shims.go`, used at `server.go:1160`), `drainerShim{s}` (`nomad/drainer_shims.go`, used at `server.go:1194`).
2. **RPC endpoint structs** passed as interfaces — `NewDeploymentEndpoint(s, nil)`, `NewJobEndpoints(s, nil)` (`server.go:1166-1167`), `NewCSIVolumeEndpoint(s, nil)` (`server.go:1180`). These embed the server itself.

**Consumer-side interface declarations found so far** (the narrowed handles that get retained):
- `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:40` `DeploymentRaftEndpoints`; `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployment_watcher.go:37` `deploymentTriggers`
- `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:48` `RaftApplier`, `:55` `NodeTracker`; `nomad/drainer/drain_heap.go:14` `DrainDeadlineNotifier`; `nomad/drainer/watch_jobs.go:33` `DrainingJobWatcher`
- `nomad/volumewatcher/interfaces.go:14` `CSIVolumeRPC`

Other `nomad/` subpackages that are candidates for the same pattern and still need sweeping: `auth/`, `lock/`, `peers/`, `reporting/`, `state/`, `stream/`, plus in-package retainers (`worker.go`, `encrypter.go`, `heartbeat.go`, `periodic.go`, `plan_apply.go`, `blocked_evals.go`, `core_sched.go`) and out-of-package consumers in `command/agent/`.

**Next agent's starting move:** grep for `interface {` declarations under `nomad/**` and `command/agent/**` (excluding `_test.go`), then cross-reference against fields whose type is one of those interfaces — that intersection is the audit list.

content.text:
# The contract you're about to change

## 1. Construction — one giant constructor, no phases

`nomad.NewServer(config, consulCatalog, consulConfigFunc)` — `nomad/server.go:336`. It is called from exactly one production site: `command/agent/agent.go:1197`, inside `(*Agent).setupServer()` `command/agent/agent.go:1174`.

The constructor does everything in a fixed order, and **the half-built `s` is handed out to subsystems long before it is functional**. Ordered, with line numbers:

| # | Line | What happens | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `server.go:342-355` | TLS config, logger, license validate | pre-struct |
| 2 | `server.go:360-380` | `s := &Server{...}` literal — connPool, rpcServer, streamingRpcs, nodeConns, peersCache, lock TTL/delay timers, all the channels | |
| 3 | `server.go:382-384` | `s.shutdownCtx/shutdownCancel/shutdownCh` | the master cancellation token |
| 4 | `server.go:392-402` | `NewEvalBroker(s.shutdownCtx, ...)`, `NewBlockedEvals(s.evalBroker, s.logger)` | |
| 5 | `server.go:405` | `s.rpcHandler = newRpcHandler(s)` | **first retention of `s`** |
| 6 | `server.go:408-412` | `newPlanner(s)` | retains `s` |
| 7 | `server.go:415` | `newNodeHeartbeater(s)` | retains `s` |
| 8 | `server.go:418` | `NewPeriodicDispatch(s.logger, s)` | retains `s` via narrowed iface |
| 9 | `server.go:421-422` | `NewStatsFetcher(...)`, `statsFetcher.SetLocalServer(s)` | retains `s` |
| 10 | `server.go:425` | `NewConsulConfigsAPI` | |
| 11 | `server.go:428-440` | `NewEncrypter(s, keystorePath)` | retains `s`; keystore on disk |
| 12 | `server.go:444-462` | OIDC discovery, `oidcProviderCache`, `oidcRequestCache` | background resources |
| 13 | `server.go:465` | `setupRPC(tlsWrap)` → `setupRpcServer` `server.go:1296` registers **~30 endpoint structs each holding `s`** | |
| 14 | `server.go:475-483` | `auth.NewAuthenticator` with method-value closures `s.State`, `s.getLeaderAcl` | **closures pin `s`** |

## 2. Shutdown — nil-guarded, no reversal of the retention graph

Order: `shutdownCancel()` `:754` → stop workers + wait with `workerShutdownGracePeriod` `:756-761` → `serf.Shutdown()` `:763` → raft transport/layer/raft/store close `:767-777` → `rpcListener.Close()` `:780` → `connPool.Shutdown()` `:785` → `fsm.Close()` `:788` → `consulConfigEntries.Stop()` `:793` → `oidcProviderCache.Shutdown()` `:796`.

## 3. What the server owns that outlives a single request

- **Durable/replicated state:** `fsm` (`:145`), and through it the state store — reached via `(*Server).State() *state.StateStore` `nomad/server.go:2115` (`return s.fsm.State()`). `raft`, `raftLayer`, `raftStore`, `raftInmem`, `raftTransport` (`:139-143`).
- **Cluster membership:** `serf` (`:189`), `peersCache` (`:196`), `reconcileCh` (`:202`), `eventCh`.
- **Network:** `rpcListener` + `listenerCh` (`:147-148`), `rpcServer` (`:161`), `connPool` (`:134`), `streamingRpcs` (`:176`), `nodeConns`/`nodeConnsLock` (`:180-181`), `tlsWrap`/`tlsWrapLock` (`:152-153`), `rpcTLS`/`rpcCancel` (`:170-171`).
- **Scheduling machinery:** `evalBroker`, `blockedEvals`, `planner`/`planQueue`, `workers` + `workerLock` + `workerShutdownGroup`, `periodicDispatcher`, `nodeHeartbeater`.
- **Leader-only subsystems (created once, enabled/disabled per leadership):** `deploymentWatcher`, `volumeWatcher`, `nodeDrainer`, `keyringReplicator`, `volumeControllerFutures`.
- **Security/crypto:** `encrypter` (keystore-backed, `:428`), `auth` (`:475`), `oidcProviderCache`, `oidcRequestCache`, `oidcDisco`.
- **Lifecycle primitives:** `shutdownCtx`/`shutdownCancel`/`shutdownCh`/`shutdown`/`shutdownLock`, `bootstrapped`, `readyForConsistentReads`, `left`, `reassertLeaderCh`.

## 4. Layers between an incoming RPC and that state

Bottom to top, each with its role in one phrase:

1. **`net.Listener`** — `createRPCListener` `nomad/server.go:592`; raw TCP accept, socket owned by `s.rpcListener`.
2. **`(*rpcHandler).listen(ctx)`** — `nomad/rpc.go:196`; accept loop + per-remote-address connection limiting, started by `startRPCListener` `nomad/server.go:588`.
3. **`(*rpcHandler).handleConn`** — `nomad/rpc.go:280`; reads the one-byte RPC type, does TLS upgrade, builds the per-connection `*RPCContext`.
4. **`handleMultiplex` / `handleMultiplexV2`** — `nomad/rpc.go:418` / `:524`; yamux session demux so one TCP conn carries many logical RPCs.
5. **`(*rpcHandler).handleNomadConn`** — `nomad/rpc.go:457`; feeds decoded frames into `net/rpc`'s `*rpc.Server`.
6. **`*rpc.Server` dispatch table** — populated by `setupRpcServer` `nomad/server.go:1296`; maps `"Job.Register"` → the method on a registered endpoint struct. **This is the retention chokepoint: ~30 endpoint structs (`server.go:1298-1341`), each constructed with `s` and each holding it for the process lifetime.**
7. **`rpcHandler` struct** — `nomad/rpc.go:47`, holds `srv *Server` at `:48`; the single most important retained handle.
8. **`(*rpcHandler).forward`** — `nomad/rpc.go:586`, with `forwardLeader` `:685`, `forwardServer` `:694`, `forwardRegion` `:716`; region/leader redirection before any local work.
9. **Authentication** — `(*Server).Authenticate` `nomad/acl.go:11` (plus `AuthenticateServerOnly` `:15`, `AuthenticateNodeIdentityGenerator` `:19`, `AuthenticateClientOnly` `:23`), delegating to `s.auth`, the `*auth.Authenticator` built at `nomad/server.go:475` from `s.State`/`s.getLeaderAcl` **method-value closures**.
10. **Read path — `(*rpcHandler).blockingRPC`** — `nomad/rpc.go:854` (options type `:846`); watch-set-driven blocking queries against the state store, e.g. `nomad/acl_endpoint.go:249`.
11. **Write path — `(*Server).raftApply`** — `nomad/rpc.go:811` (future variant `:790`); serializes the message and applies through Raft.
12. **FSM** — `nomad/fsm.go`; applies committed log entries.
13. **`*state.StateStore`** — reached via `(*Server).State()` `nomad/server.go:2115`; the in-memory memdb that is the actual state.

## 5. Reload — the existing, and very partial, re-wiring path

`(*Server).Reload(newConfig)` `nomad/server.go:910`, called from `(*Agent).Reload` `command/agent/agent.go:1642`. It handles only four things: TLS (`reloadTLSConnections`, `:922`), enterprise license (`:930`), scheduler worker pool resize (`:937-943`), and raft reloadable config (`:945-953`).

Two things worth flagging for the rework: it **mutates in place and never replaces `s`**, so no retained handle has ever needed to be re-pointed; and `reloadSchedulers(s, newVals)` is called twice at `server.go:940` and `:942` — the second call is outside the `newVals.IsValid()` guard, which looks like a bug independent of your work.

## Bottom line for the rework

The contract today is: **`*Server` is created once, handed out eagerly and partially-initialized to ~40 retainers, mutated in place forever, and torn down by cancelling one context without ever telling those retainers.** Replacing or restarting the server means every site in §1 steps 5–25 and every entry in `setupRpcServer` `server.go:1296-1341` is a place a stale handle can survive. Task 3's audit of retained narrowed handles is the enumeration of exactly that set.

}

content.text:
I have the full picture. Here is the trace, one hop at a time.

# How server access reaches a subsystem

There are **five structurally distinct handover mechanisms** in this codebase. Each narrows access differently, and each leaves a different kind of retained handle. I'll trace each end to end.

---

## Mechanism A — Raft-only shim over a bound method value
### Trace: `deploymentwatcher.Watcher` → Raft writes

| Hop | Location | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Subsystem declares its need | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:40-68` | `type DeploymentRaftEndpoints interface` — exactly 5 methods: `UpsertJob`, `UpdateDeploymentStatus`, `UpdateDeploymentPromotion`, `UpdateDeploymentAllocHealth`, `UpdateAllocDesiredTransition`. No mention of `*Server`. |
| 2. Constructor signature demands it | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:103-110` | `NewDeploymentsWatcher(logger, raft DeploymentRaftEndpoints, deploymentRPC DeploymentRPC, jobRPC JobRPC, stateQueriesPerSecond, updateBatchDuration)` |
| 3. Server builds the shim | `nomad/server.go:1160-1162` | `raftShim := &deploymentWatcherRaftShim{apply: s.raftApply}` — **narrowing happens here**, and it narrows to a *single bound method value*, not to `s` |
| 4. Shim type holds only a func | `nomad/deployment_watcher_shims.go:13-16` | `type deploymentWatcherRaftShim struct { apply raftApplyFn }` |
| 5. Shim satisfies the interface | `nomad/deployment_watcher_shims.go:30-58` | Five methods, each wrapping `d.apply(structs.XRequestType, msg)` + `convertApplyErrors` (`:22`) |
| 6. Handover | `nomad/server.go:1165-1172` | `s.deploymentWatcher = deploymentwatcher.NewDeploymentsWatcher(s.logger, raftShim, NewDeploymentEndpoint(s, nil), NewJobEndpoints(s, nil), ...)` |
| 7. **Retention** | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:80` | `raft DeploymentRaftEndpoints` — field on `Watcher`, assigned at `:112`, held for the process lifetime |

**Why this one matters most:** hop 3 captures `s.raftApply` as a *bound method value*. That closure pins `s` permanently, and — critically — **it cannot be re-pointed**. If you replace the server, `raftShim.apply` still calls the old `s.raftApply`, and there is no setter anywhere to fix it. The narrowing that looks like the cleanest decoupling is actually the most rigid binding.

Note also hop 6 passes `nil` as the `*RPCContext` — these endpoint instances are *second, watcher-private copies* of `Deployment` and `Job`, distinct from the ones registered on the RPC server at `nomad/server.go:1318` and `:1320`. Same `s`, two retention paths.

**CE/ENT wrinkle:** `DeploymentRPC` and `JobRPC` are `interface{}` in CE — `nomad/deploymentwatcher/multiregion_ce.go:13-14` — so hop 6's narrowing is a no-op in CE and a real narrowing in Enterprise. Retained at `deployments_watcher.go:82,85`, re-retained per-deployment by embedding at `deployment_watcher.go:69,73`, assigned `:125-126`.

---

## Mechanism B — Struct shim wrapping the whole server
### Trace: `drainer.NodeDrainer` → Raft writes

| Hop | Location | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Subsystem declares its need | `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:48-51` | `type RaftApplier interface { AllocUpdateDesiredTransition(...); NodesDrainComplete(...) }` — 2 methods |
| 2. Config struct carries it | `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:114-131` | `NodeDrainerConfig{ Logger, Raft RaftApplier, JobFactory, NodeFactory, DrainDeadlineFactory, ... }` |
| 3. Server builds the shim | `nomad/server.go:1189` | `shim := drainerShim{s}` — **narrowing is only at the type level; the shim holds the entire `*Server`** |
| 4. Shim type | `nomad/drainer_shims.go:13-15` | `type drainerShim struct { s *Server }` |
| 5. Shim methods reach deep into `s` | `nomad/drainer_shims.go:18-35`, `:37-45` | Both read `d.s.config.Region` **and** call `d.s.raftApply(...)` — two independent dependencies on live server state |
| 6. Handover | `nomad/server.go:1190-1199` | config literal with `Raft: shim`, then `s.nodeDrainer = drainer.NewNodeDrainer(c)` at `:1200` |
| 7. **Retention** | `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:159` | `raft RaftApplier` field on `NodeDrainer`, assigned `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:177` |

**Contrast with A:** the drainer's shim reads `d.s` at *call* time, so if you could swap the pointer inside `drainerShim` the subsystem would follow. The deployment watcher's shim cannot. Same "narrow the interface" idiom, opposite reworkability.

Sibling interfaces in the same package, narrowed away from the server entirely and given `*state.StateStore` directly via factories: `NodeTracker` `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:55-66`, `DrainingJobWatcher` `nomad/drainer/watch_jobs.go:33`, `DrainDeadlineNotifier` `nomad/drainer/drain_heap.go:14`, with factory types at `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:69-77` and constructors `GetDrainingJobWatcher` `:80`, `GetDeadlineNotifier` `:85`, `GetNodeWatcherFactory` `:90`. These are wired in at `nomad/server.go:1192-1194`.

---

## Mechanism C — RPC endpoint struct passed as a one-method interface
### Trace: `volumewatcher.Watcher` → CSI unpublish RPC

| Hop | Location | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Subsystem declares its need | `nomad/volumewatcher/interfaces.go:14-16` | `type CSIVolumeRPC interface { Unpublish(args, reply) error }` — **one method**. The doc comment at `:10-13` states the intent outright: "a minimal interface of the Server... to avoid circular references between the nomad package and the volumewatcher" |
| 2. Constructor demands it | `nomad/volumewatcher/volumes_watcher.go:52` | `NewVolumesWatcher(logger log.Logger, rpc CSIVolumeRPC, leaderAcl string)` |
| 3. Server narrows by constructing an endpoint | `nomad/server.go:1180` | `NewCSIVolumeEndpoint(s, nil)` — the endpoint struct *is* the narrowing device; it holds `srv *Server` and happens to expose `Unpublish` |
| 4. Handover | `nomad/server.go:1179-1180` | `s.volumeWatcher = volumewatcher.NewVolumesWatcher(s.logger, NewCSIVolumeEndpoint(s, nil), s.getLeaderAcl())` |
| 5. **Retention** | `nomad/volumewatcher/volumes_watcher.go:26` | `rpc CSIVolumeRPC` field, assigned `:60` |

**Two extra hazards here.** First, `s.getLeaderAcl()` is **called** at hop 4 and its *string result* is retained at `volumes_watcher.go:29` (`leaderAcl string`, assigned `:64`) — a snapshot, not a live accessor. Second, the constructor at `volumes_watcher.go:57` builds its own `context.WithCancel(context.Background())` — deliberately **not** derived from `s.shutdownCtx`. There are **five structurally distinct handover mechanisms** in this codebase. Each narrows access differently, and each leaves a different kind of retained handle. I'll trace each end to end.

---

## Mechanism A — Raft-only shim over a bound method value
### Trace: `deploymentwatcher.Watcher` → Raft writes

| Hop | Location | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Subsystem declares its need | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:40-68` | `type DeploymentRaftEndpoints interface` — exactly 5 methods: `UpsertJob`, `UpdateDeploymentStatus`, `UpdateDeploymentPromotion`, `UpdateDeploymentAllocHealth`, `UpdateAllocDesiredTransition`. No mention of `*Server`. |
| 2. Constructor signature demands it | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:103-110` | `NewDeploymentsWatcher(logger, raft DeploymentRaftEndpoints, deploymentRPC DeploymentRPC, jobRPC JobRPC, stateQueriesPerSecond, updateBatchDuration)` |
| 3. Server builds the shim | `nomad/server.go:1160-1162` | `raftShim := &deploymentWatcherRaftShim{apply: s.raftApply}` — **narrowing happens here**, and it narrows to a *single bound method value*, not to `s` |
| 4. Shim type holds only a func | `nomad/deployment_watcher_shims.go:13-16` | `type deploymentWatcherRaftShim struct { apply raftApplyFn }` |
| 5. Shim satisfies the interface | `nomad/deployment_watcher_shims.go:30-58` | Five methods, each wrapping `d.apply(structs.XRequestType, msg)` + `convertApplyErrors` (`:22`) |
| 6. Handover | `nomad/server.go:1165-1172` | `s.deploymentWatcher = deploymentwatcher.NewDeploymentsWatcher(s.logger, raftShim, NewDeploymentEndpoint(s, nil), NewJobEndpoints(s, nil), ...)` |
| 7. **Retention** | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:80` | `raft DeploymentRaftEndpoints` — field on `Watcher`, assigned at `:112`, held for the process lifetime |

**Why this one matters most:** hop 3 captures `s.raftApply` as a *bound method value*. That closure pins `s` permanently, and — critically — **it cannot be re-pointed**. If you replace the server, `raftShim.apply` still calls the old `s.raftApply`, and there is no setter anywhere to fix it. The narrowing that looks like the cleanest decoupling is actually the most rigid binding.

Note also hop 6 passes `nil` as the `*RPCContext` — these endpoint instances are *second, watcher-private copies* of `Deployment` and `Job`, distinct from the ones registered on the RPC server at `nomad/server.go:1318` and `:1320`. Same `s`, two retention paths.

**CE/ENT wrinkle:** `DeploymentRPC` and `JobRPC` are `interface{}` in CE — `nomad/deploymentwatcher/multiregion_ce.go:13-14` — so hop 6's narrowing is a no-op in CE and a real narrowing in Enterprise. Retained at `deployments_watcher.go:82,85`, re-retained per-deployment by embedding at `deployment_watcher.go:69,73`, assigned `:125-126`.

---

## Mechanism B — Struct shim wrapping the whole server
### Trace: `drainer.NodeDrainer` → Raft writes

| Hop | Location | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Subsystem declares its need | `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:48-51` | `type RaftApplier interface { AllocUpdateDesiredTransition(...); NodesDrainComplete(...) }` — 2 methods |
| 2. Config struct carries it | `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:114-131` | `NodeDrainerConfig{ Logger, Raft RaftApplier, JobFactory, NodeFactory, DrainDeadlineFactory, ... }` |
| 3. Server builds the shim | `nomad/server.go:1189` | `shim := drainerShim{s}` — **narrowing is only at the type level; the shim holds the entire `*Server`** |
| 4. Shim type | `nomad/drainer_shims.go:13-15` | `type drainerShim struct { s *Server }` |

type RPCer interface {
	RPC(string, any, any) error
	Server() *nomad.Server
	Client() *client.Client
	Stats() map[string]map[string]string
	GetConfig() *Config
	GetMetricsSink() *metrics.InmemSink
	ConfigReload() error
}

// HTTPServer is used to wrap an Agent and expose it over an HTTP interface

	agent RPCer

	// eventAuditor is the enterprise audit log feature which is needed by the
	// HTTP server.
	eventAuditor event.Auditor

	mux        *http.ServeMux
	listener   net.Listener
	listenerCh chan struct{}

content.text:
# Audit: every production holder of a live handle to `*nomad.Server`

Method: swept for (a) all struct fields typed `*Server`, (b) every locally-declared `interface`/`func` type in the tree and every struct field typed with one, (c) transitive holders via server-owned objects. Excluded `_test.go`, `nomad/mock/`, `scheduler/tests/`, `nomad/testing.go`, `client/config/testing.go`.

**Total: 63 production holders.** Legend for the "reach" column: **D** = direct `*Server` field; **N** = narrowed interface field (the easy-to-miss kind — file never names `*Server`); **C** = captured closure/bound method value; **V** = copied value snapshot; **T** = transitive through another holder.

---

## Area 1 — RPC transport (1)

| # | Retaining field | Reach | How it gets there |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `nomad/rpc.go:48` — `rpcHandler.srv` | D | `newRpcHandler(s)` `nomad/rpc.go:73`, assigned `:76`, from `nomad/server.go:405`; also **embedded into `Server` itself** at `nomad/server.go:157` |

---

## Area 2 — RPC endpoint structs (30)

All constructed in `setupRpcServer` `nomad/server.go:1296-1341` and registered on the `*rpc.Server` dispatch table; each holds `srv *Server` for the process lifetime. All reach **D**.

| # | Retaining field | Type |
|---|---|---|
| 2 | `nomad/acl_endpoint.go:66` | `ACL` |
| 3 | `nomad/alloc_endpoint.go:25` | `Alloc` |
| 4 | `nomad/client_agent_endpoint.go:27` | `Agent` |
| 5 | `nomad/client_alloc_endpoint.go:26` | `ClientAllocations` |
| 6 | `nomad/client_csi_endpoint.go:24` | `ClientCSI` |
| 7 | `nomad/client_fs_endpoint.go:26` | `FileSystem` |
| 8 | `nomad/client_host_volume_endpoint.go:18` | `ClientHostVolume` |
| 9 | `nomad/client_identity_endpoint.go:14` | `NodeIdentity` |
| 10 | `nomad/client_meta_endpoint.go:16` | `NodeMeta` |
| 11 | `nomad/client_stats_endpoint.go:19` | `ClientStats` |
| 12 | `nomad/csi_endpoint.go:30` | `CSIVolume` |
| 13 | `nomad/csi_endpoint.go:1820` | `CSIPlugin` |
| 14 | `nomad/deployment_endpoint.go:23` | `Deployment` |
| 15 | `nomad/eval_endpoint.go:35` | `Eval` |
| 16 | `nomad/event_endpoint.go:21` | `Event` |
| 17 | `nomad/host_volume_endpoint.go:30` | `HostVolume` |
| 18 | `nomad/job_endpoint.go:51` | `Job` |
| 19 | `nomad/keyring_endpoint.go:23` | `Keyring` |
| 20 | `nomad/namespace_endpoint.go:20` | `Namespace` |
| 21 | `nomad/node_endpoint.go:60` | `Node` |
| 22 | `nomad/node_pool_endpoint.go:23` | `NodePool` |
| 23 | `nomad/operator_endpoint.go:31` | `Operator` |
| 24 | `nomad/periodic_endpoint.go:20` | `Periodic` |
| 25 | `nomad/plan_endpoint.go:18` | `Plan` |
| 26 | `nomad/regions_endpoint.go:14` | `Region` |
| 27 | `nomad/scaling_endpoint.go:20` | `Scaling` |
| 28 | `nomad/search_endpoint.go:50` | `Search` |
| 29 | `nomad/service_registration_endpoint.go:30` | `ServiceRegistration` |
| 30 | `nomad/status_endpoint.go:18` | `Status` |
| 31 | `nomad/system_endpoint.go:16` | `System` |
| 32 | `nomad/task_group_host_volume_claim_endpoint.go:23` | `TaskGroupHostVolumeClaim` |

**Duplicate-instance hazard:** four of these are constructed a *second* time outside the dispatch table, with `nil` ctx, and handed to long-lived subsystems — `NewDeploymentEndpoint(s, nil)` and `NewJobEndpoints(s, nil)` at `nomad/server.go:1166-1167`, `NewCSIVolumeEndpoint(s, nil)` at `nomad/server.go:1180`, and `NewStatusEndpoint(f.localServer, nil)` constructed **per call** at `nomad/stats_fetcher.go:72`. Fixing the registry copy will not fix these.

---

## Area 3 — Job admission controllers (10)

All built inside the `Job` endpoint constructor `nomad/job_endpoint.go:61-89`, retained in `Job.mutators` `nomad/job_endpoint.go:56` and `Job.validators` `nomad/job_endpoint.go:57` (slices typed `[]jobMutator` / `[]jobValidator`, interfaces declared `nomad/job_endpoint_hooks.go:168` and `:173`). Each individual hook struct holds its own `srv`:

| # | Retaining field | Type | Reach |
|---|---|---|---|
| 33 | `nomad/job_endpoint_hooks.go:234` | `jobCanonicalizer.srv` | D |
| 34 | `nomad/job_endpoint_hooks.go:483` | `jobValidate.srv` | D |
| 35 | `nomad/job_endpoint_hooks.go:618` | `memoryOversubscriptionValidate.srv` | D |
| 36 | `nomad/job_endpoint_hook_vault.go:14` | `jobVaultHook.srv` | D — registered twice, as mutator `job_endpoint.go:65` and validator `:76` |
| 37 | `nomad/job_endpoint_hook_consul.go:15` | `jobConsulHook.srv` | D — registered twice, `job_endpoint.go:66` and `:77` |
| 38 | `nomad/job_endpoint_hook_node_pool.go:15` | `jobNodePoolMutatingHook.srv` | D |
| 39 | `nomad/job_endpoint_hook_node_pool_ce.go:19` | `jobNodePoolValidatingHook.srv` | D |
| 40 | `nomad/job_endpoint_hook_implicit_identities.go:13` | `jobImplicitIdentitiesHook.srv` | D |
| 41 | `nomad/job_endpoint_validators.go:13` | `jobNamespaceConstraintCheckHook.srv` | D |
| 42 | `nomad/job_endpoint.go:56-57` | `Job.mutators` / `Job.validators` slices | N — the slices themselves are typed as local interfaces; nothing here names `*Server` |

Because the `Job` endpoint is constructed twice (Area 2 note), **every one of these hooks exists in two live copies**.

---

## Area 4 — Scheduling (7)

| # | Retaining field | Reach | How it gets there |
|---|---|---|---|
| 43 | `nomad/worker.go:93` — `Worker.srv` | D | one `*Worker` per scheduler goroutine, created in `setupWorkersLocked` `nomad/server.go:1944`; retained in `Server.workers` |
| 44 | `nomad/core_sched.go:28` — `CoreScheduler.srv` | D | `NewCoreScheduler(w.srv, snap, w)` `nomad/worker.go:626` |
| 45 | `nomad/core_sched.go:35` — `CoreScheduler.planner` | N | typed `sstructs.Planner` (`scheduler/structs/interfaces.go:108`); the value passed is `w`, the `*Worker` — so the server is reachable twice from this one struct |
| 46 | `scheduler/generic_sched.go:57` — `GenericScheduler.planner` | N+T | typed `sstructs.Planner`, satisfied by `*Worker` (`SubmitPlan` `nomad/worker.go:651`, `UpdateEval` `:731`, `CreateEval` `:770`, `ReblockEval` `:812`, `ServersMeetMinimumVersion` `:645`); wired at `nomad/worker.go:628`. **`scheduler/` never imports the `nomad` package** |
| 47 | `scheduler/scheduler_system.go:37` — `SystemScheduler.planner` | N+T | same, via `scheduler.NewScheduler` `scheduler/scheduler.go:33` |
| 48 | `scheduler/scheduler_sysbatch.go:33` — `SysBatchScheduler.planner` | N+T | same |
| 49 | `nomad/plan_apply.go:25` — `planner.srv` | D | `newPlanner(s)` `nomad/plan_apply.go:39` ← `nomad/server.go:408` |

Holders 46-48 are the single most-missable group in the codebase: three structs in a package with no dependency on `nomad` at all, each keeping the leader's server alive through a five-method interface.

Checked and **excluded**: `scheduler/structs/structs.go:76` `PlanBuilder.Planner` — both production construction sites (`nomad/job_endpoint.go:1892` for `Job.Plan`, `nomad/fsm.go:2120` for snapshot-restore queued-alloc recalculation) leave it `nil` and set only `State`. Not a holder today, but it is a loaded gun: `PlanBuilder.SubmitPlan` `scheduler/structs/structs.go:103-104` dispatches to it if ever set.

---

## Area 5 — Leader-only watchers (9)

### Deployment watcher
| # | Retaining field | Reach | How it gets there |
|---|---|---|---|
| 50 | `nomad/deployment_watcher_shims.go:15` — `deploymentWatcherRaftShim.apply` | **C** | `raftApplyFn` (`nomad/rpc.go:806`) bound from `s.raftApply` at `nomad/server.go:1161`. **Cannot be re-pointed** — no `*Server` to swap, just a frozen closure |
| 51 | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:76` — `Watcher.raft` | N | `DeploymentRaftEndpoints` (`:40`), assigned `:112` from holder 50 |
| 52 | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/batcher.go:20` — `AllocUpdateBatcher.raft` | N+T | same interface, second retention created per-batcher |
| 53 | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:82` — `Watcher.deploymentRPC` | N | `DeploymentRPC` — `interface{}` in CE (`multiregion_ce.go:13`), real in ENT; value is the `Deployment` endpoint from `nomad/server.go:1166` |
| 54 | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:85` — `Watcher.jobRPC` | N | `JobRPC` (`multiregion_ce.go:14`); value from `nomad/server.go:1167` |
| 55 | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployment_watcher.go:65` — `deploymentWatcher.deploymentTriggers` (**embedded**) | N+T | `deploymentTriggers` (`:37`), assigned `:125`; satisfied by the parent `*Watcher`, which holds holder 51. One instance **per active deployment** |
| 56 | `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployment_watcher.go:69` + `:73` — embedded `DeploymentRPC` / `JobRPC` | N+T | assigned `:125-126`, per-deployment re-retention of holders 53-54 |

### Node drainer
| # | Retaining field | Reach | How it gets there |
|---|---|---|---|
| 57 | `nomad/drainer_shims.go:15` — `drainerShim.s` | D | `drainerShim{s}` `nomad/server.go:1189`; dereferences `d.s.config.Region` and `d.s.raftApply` at call time (`drainer_shims.go:22,33,42`) |
| 58 | `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:159` — `NodeDrainer.raft` | N | `RaftApplier` (`:48`), assigned `:177` via `NodeDrainerConfig.Raft` (`:116`) from `nomad/server.go:1191` |
| 59 | `nomad/drainer/watch_nodes.go:130` — `nodeDrainWatcher.tracker` | N+T | `NodeTracker` (`nomad/drainer/drainer.go:55`), satisfied by `*NodeDrainer` → holder 58 |

### Volume watcher
| # | Retaining field | Reach | How it gets there |
|---|---|---|---|
| 60 | `nomad/volumewatcher/volumes_watcher.go:26` — `Watcher.rpc` | N | `CSIVolumeRPC` (`interfaces.go:14`, one method); value is `NewCSIVolumeEndpoint(s, nil)` `nomad/server.go:1180` |
| 61 | `nomad/volumewatcher/volume_watcher.go:30` — `volumeWatcher.rpc` | N+T | same interface, per-volume re-retention |

Plus two **value snapshots** that go stale silently rather than pointing at a dead server: `nomad/volumewatcher/volumes_watcher.go:29` (`leaderAcl string`, from `s.getLeaderAcl()` **invoked** at `nomad/server.go:1180`) and its per-volume copy `nomad/volumewatcher/volume_watcher.go:33`.

Excluded after checking: `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:140,145,149` (`nodeWatcher`, `jobWatcher`, `deadlineNotifier`) — built by factories `nomad/server.go:1192-1194` that take `*state.StateStore` and `context.Context` only; they never see the server.

---

## Area 6 — Keyring, auth, crypto (7)

## What this means for the rework

Sorting the 80 holders by whether a replaced server can actually be re-pointed:

**Re-pointable in principle** (~72) — the holder dereferences a `*Server` field at call time, or an interface value backed by one. Every Area 2 endpoint, every Area 3 hook, `drainerShim`, `Worker`, `Encrypter`, `AutopilotDelegate`, `nodeHeartbeater`, `rpcHandler`. A rework could give these a shared indirection cell. `StatsFetcher` (#71) already has the setter and is the model to copy.

**Structurally frozen** (3) — closures captured at construction, with no `*Server` left to swap: `deploymentWatcherRaftShim.apply` (#50, ← `nomad/server.go:1161`), `Authenticator.getState` (#65, ← `:477`), `Authenticator.getLeaderACL` (#66, ← `:478`). These require changing the *shape* of the handover, not just adding a setter.

**Stale-data holders** (5) — no pointer at all, just copied values that were true of a server that no longer exists: `volumewatcher` `leaderAcl` at `volumes_watcher.go:29` and `volume_watcher.go:33`; `auth` `region`/`aclsEnabled`/cert-name slices at `auth.go:54,44,56-57`. These fail *silently* — no nil panic, just wrong authorization decisions.

**Multiplied holders** — three subsystems create a fresh retention per work item, so the real count at runtime is unbounded: `deploymentWatcher` (#55-56) one per active deployment, `AllocUpdateBatcher` (#52) one per watcher, `volumeWatcher` (#61) one per volume, plus the `Job`/`Deployment`/`CSIVolume` endpoints that exist in two copies each (`nomad/server.go:1166-1167`, `:1180` vs. the dispatch table).

And the gap that makes all of this ship as dead handles: `(*Server).Shutdown()` `nomad/server.go:744` cancels `shutdownCtx` and closes I/O but **de-registers nothing** — not one of the 80 fields above is cleared, notified, or invalidated. `(*Server).Reload()` `nomad/server.go:910` touches only TLS, license, worker-pool size, and raft config.

Excluded `_test.go`, `nomad/mock/`, `scheduler/tests/`, `nomad/testing.go`, `client/config/testing.go`.

**Total: 63 production holders.** Legend for the "reach" column: **D** = direct `*Server` field; **N** = narrowed interface field (the easy-to-miss kind — file never names `*Server`); **C** = captured closure/bound method value; **V** = copied value snapshot; **T** = transitive through another holder.

Raft can also **replace the FSM's state store wholesale** during `Restore()` (see the `stateLock` comment `nomad/fsm.go:150-153`), so any rework must decide whether a restore counts as a server-identity change.

---

## Area 10 — Agent / HTTP layer (3)

| # | Retaining field | Reach | How it gets there |
|---|---|---|---|
| 78 | `command/agent/agent.go:113` — `Agent.server` | D | `nomad.NewServer(...)` `command/agent/agent.go:1197` in `setupServer()` `:1174`. **The root of the whole retention graph** — the only production construction site |
| 79 | `command/agent/http.go:102` — `HTTPServer.agent` | **N** | typed `RPCer` (`command/agent/http.go:90-99`), whose method set includes `Server() *nomad.Server`; satisfied by `*Agent` (`Server()` at `command/agent/agent.go:1566`). One `HTTPServer` per configured HTTP address, built by `NewHTTPServers` `command/agent/http.go:119` |
| 80 | `command/agent/command.go:59` — `Command.agent` | T | `*Agent` → holder 78 |

Holder 79 is the out-of-`nomad`-package instance of exactly the pattern the ticket calls out — a locally-declared interface whose method set hands out the server, retained in a field that never names the server type. Note it is reachable statically too: `aclPermissionCheckHelper(srv *nomad.Server, ...)` `command/agent/agent_endpoint.go:1002`.

---

## Area 11 — Latent holder: declared, wired only in tests

| Field | Status |
|---|---|
| `client/config/config.go:161` — `Config.RPCHandler`, typed `RPCHandler` (`client/config/config.go:83`, one method `RPC`) | **Never assigned anywhere in production** — a repo-wide sweep finds only the declaration, the doc comment ("can be provided to the Client if there is a local server to avoid going over the network"), and the read site `client/rpc.go:78-80`. `*nomad.Server` satisfies it. Today it is dead; if the rework wires it up to short-circuit client→server RPC in a combined agent, it becomes holder #81 and it is retained inside a `client/config.Config` that is **copied and handed to every alloc runner and task runner** |

---

## What this means for the rework

Sorting the 80 holders by whether a replaced server can actually be re-pointed:

**Re-pointable in principle** (~72) — the holder dereferences a `*Server` field at call time, or an interface value backed by one. Every Area 2 endpoint, every Area 3 hook, `drainerShim`, `Worker`, `Encrypter`, `AutopilotDelegate`, `nodeHeartbeater`, `rpcHandler`. A rework could give these a shared indirection cell. `StatsFetcher` (#71) already has the setter and is the model to copy.

**Structurally frozen** (3) — closures captured at construction, with no `*Server` left to swap: `deploymentWatcherRaftShim.apply` (#50, ← `nomad/server.go:1161`), `Authenticator.getState` (#65, ← `:477`), `Authenticator.getLeaderACL` (#66, ← `:478`). These require changing the *shape* of the handover, not just adding a setter.

**Stale-data holders** (5) — no pointer at all, just copied values that were true of a server that no longer exists: `volumewatcher` `leaderAcl` at `volumes_watcher.go:29` and `volume_watcher.go:33`; `auth` `region`/`aclsEnabled`/cert-name slices at `auth.go:54,44,56-57`. These fail *silently* — no nil panic, just wrong authorization decisions.

**Multiplied holders** — three subsystems create a fresh retention per work item, so the real count at runtime is unbounded: `deploymentWatcher` (#55-56) one per active deployment, `AllocUpdateBatcher` (#52) one per watcher, `volumeWatcher` (#61) one per volume, plus the `Job`/`Deployment`/`CSIVolume` endpoints that exist in two copies each (`nomad/server.go:1166-1167`, `:1180` vs. the dispatch table).

And the gap that makes all of this ship as dead handles: `(*Server).Shutdown()` `nomad/server.go:744` cancels `shutdownCtx` and closes I/O but **de-registers nothing** — not one of the 80 fields above is cleared, notified, or invalidated. `(*Server).Reload()` `nomad/server.go:910` touches only TLS, license, worker-pool size, and raft config.

grep -n "func (w \*Watcher) createUpdate\|func (w \*Watcher) upsertJob\|func (w \*Watcher) upsertDeploymentStatusUpdate\|func (w \*Watcher) upsertDeploymentPromotion\|func (w \*Watcher) upsertDeploymentAllocHealth\|newDeploymentWatcher(" nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go | head -20

sed -n '300,310p' nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go; echo "=== batcher ctor ==="; grep -n "NewAllocUpdateBatcher\|allocUpdateBatcher =" nomad/deploymentwatcher/*.go | grep -v _test; echo "=== volumewatcher per-vol ==="; grep -n "newVolumeWatcher(" nomad/volumewatcher/*.go | grep -v _test; echo "=== nodeDrainWatcher ctor ==="; grep -n "nodeFactory(\|NewNodeDrainWatcher(" nomad/drainer/*.go | grep -v _test

sed -n '57,75p' nomad/volumewatcher/volume_watcher.go; echo "=== VerifyClaim ==="; grep -n "func (e \*Encrypter) VerifyClaim" nomad/encrypter.go; echo "=== Agent RPCer methods ==="; grep -n "func (a \*Agent) RPC(\|func (a \*Agent) Server()\|func (a \*Agent) Client()\|func (a \*Agent) Stats()\|func (a \*Agent) GetConfig()\|func (a \*Agent) GetMetricsSink()\|func (a \*Agent) ConfigReload()" command/agent/agent.go

content.text:
I have every link verified. Here are the mechanical traces.

# Indirect-route traces: how `*Server` gets behind a non-`*Server` field

Every holder from Task 4 whose field type is **not** `*Server`. Each trace runs: **field → interface/type declaration → what satisfies it → assignment site → chain down to `*Server`.**

---

## Route 1 — `PeriodicDispatch.dispatcher`

- **Field:** `nomad/periodic.go:27` — `dispatcher JobEvalDispatcher`
- **Interface:** `nomad/periodic.go:41-48` — `JobEvalDispatcher`, 2 methods: `DispatchJob(*structs.Job) (*structs.Evaluation, error)`, `RunningChildren(*structs.Job) (bool, error)`
- **Implementer:** `*Server` **directly**, in the same file — `func (s *Server) DispatchJob` `nomad/periodic.go:52`, `func (s *Server) RunningChildren` `nomad/periodic.go:87`
- **Assignment:** parameter `dispatcher` at `nomad/periodic.go:140` → stored `nomad/periodic.go:142`
- **Call site:** `nomad/server.go:418` — `NewPeriodicDispatch(s.logger, s)`
- **Chain:** `dispatcher` **is** `s`. One hop. Interface exists for testability only (same package, no import cycle to break).

---

## Route 2 — `Variables.timers`

- **Field:** `nomad/variables_endpoint.go:52` — `timers variableTimers`
- **Interface:** `nomad/variables_endpoint.go:39-43` — `variableTimers`, 3 methods: `CreateVariableLockTTLTimer`, `RemoveVariableLockTTLTimer`, `RenewTTLTimer`
- **Implementer:** `*Server` — `nomad/locks.go:45`, `nomad/locks.go:141`, `nomad/locks.go:114` respectively; backed by `Server.lockTTLTimer` / `Server.lockDelayTimer` (`nomad/server.go:378-379`)
- **Assignment:** `nomad/variables_endpoint.go:59` — `timers: srv` inside the `NewVariablesEndpoint` composite literal
- **Call site:** `nomad/server.go:1333` — `NewVariablesEndpoint(s, ctx, s.encrypter)`
- **Chain:** `timers` **is** `s`. The declared type names three methods; the assignment `timers: srv` is one token buried mid-literal on a line that also sets `srv`, `ctx`, `logger`, and `encrypter`.

---

## Route 3 — `Job.mutators` / `Job.validators`

- **Fields:** `nomad/job_endpoint.go:56` — `mutators []jobMutator`; `nomad/job_endpoint.go:57` — `validators []jobValidator`
- **Interfaces:** `nomad/job_endpoint_hooks.go:168-171` (`jobMutator`: `Name()`, `Mutate(*structs.Job)`), `nomad/job_endpoint_hooks.go:173-176` (`jobValidator`: `Name()`, `Validate(*structs.Job)`); both extend `admissionController` `nomad/job_endpoint_hooks.go:164`
- **Implementers holding the server:** each element struct has its own `srv *Server` field — `jobCanonicalizer` `nomad/job_endpoint_hooks.go:234`, `jobValidate` `:483`, `memoryOversubscriptionValidate` `:618`, `jobVaultHook` `nomad/job_endpoint_hook_vault.go:14`, `jobConsulHook` `nomad/job_endpoint_hook_consul.go:15`, `jobNodePoolMutatingHook` `nomad/job_endpoint_hook_node_pool.go:15`, `jobNodePoolValidatingHook` `nomad/job_endpoint_hook_node_pool_ce.go:19`, `jobImplicitIdentitiesHook` `nomad/job_endpoint_hook_implicit_identities.go:13`, `jobNamespaceConstraintCheckHook` `nomad/job_endpoint_validators.go:13`
- **Assignment:** the slice literals at `nomad/job_endpoint.go:64-73` (mutators) and `:75-86` (validators), each element written `&jobCanonicalizer{srv: s}`, `jobVaultHook{srv: s}`, …
- **Call site:** `NewJobEndpoints(s, ctx)` — registered `nomad/server.go:1320`, and again for the deployment watcher `nomad/server.go:1167`
- **Chain:** `slice element → .srv → s`. Two elements are registered in **both** slices (`jobVaultHook` at `:65` and `:77`; `jobConsulHook` at `:66` and `:77`), so each is a distinct struct value with its own copy of `s`.

---

## Route 4 — `CoreScheduler.planner`

- **Field:** `nomad/core_sched.go:35` — `planner sstructs.Planner`
- **Interface:** `scheduler/structs/interfaces.go:108-129` — `Planner`: `SubmitPlan`, `UpdateEval`, `CreateEval`, `ReblockEval`, `ServersMeetMinimumVersion`
- **Implementer:** `*Worker` — `nomad/worker.go:651` (`SubmitPlan`), `:731` (`UpdateEval`), `:770` (`CreateEval`), `:812` (`ReblockEval`), `:645` (`ServersMeetMinimumVersion`)
- **Assignment:** parameter at `nomad/core_sched.go:45` (`NewCoreScheduler(srv *Server, snap *state.StateSnapshot, planner sstructs.Planner)`)
- **Call site:** `nomad/worker.go:626` — `sched = NewCoreScheduler(w.srv, snap, w)` — the worker passes **itself** as the planner
- **Chain:** `planner → *Worker → nomad/worker.go:93 (srv) → s`. Note `CoreScheduler` reaches the server **twice**: directly via its own `srv` field `nomad/core_sched.go:28`, and indirectly via `planner`.

---

## Route 5 — `GenericScheduler.planner`

- **Field:** `scheduler/generic_sched.go:57` — `planner sstructs.Planner`
- **Interface:** `scheduler/structs/interfaces.go:108`
- **Implementer:** `*Worker`, methods as in Route 4
- **Assignment:** constructor params `NewServiceScheduler` `scheduler/generic_sched.go:80` and `NewBatchScheduler` `scheduler/generic_sched.go:92`, both reached through the factory type `Factory` `scheduler/structs/interfaces.go:20` and the dispatcher `scheduler.NewScheduler` `scheduler/scheduler.go:33`
- **Call site:** `nomad/worker.go:628` — `scheduler.NewScheduler(eval.Type, w.logger, w.srv.workersEventCh, snap, w)`
- **Chain:** `planner → *Worker → nomad/worker.go:93 → s`
- **Why it's invisible:** the `scheduler` package has **no import of `github.com/hashicorp/nomad/nomad`**. Nothing in `scheduler/generic_sched.go` mentions a server. The only textual clue is the `sstructs.Planner` type name.

## Route 6 — `SystemScheduler.planner`
- **Field:** `scheduler/scheduler_system.go:37` · **Interface:** `scheduler/structs/interfaces.go:108` · **Implementer:** `*Worker` · **Assignment:** `NewSystemScheduler` `scheduler/scheduler_system.go:62` · **Call site:** `nomad/worker.go:628` via `scheduler/scheduler.go:33` · **Chain:** → `nomad/worker.go:93` → `s`

## Route 7 — `SysBatchScheduler.planner`
- **Field:** `scheduler/scheduler_sysbatch.go:33` · **Interface:** `scheduler/structs/interfaces.go:108` · **Implementer:** `*Worker` · **Assignment:** `NewSysBatchScheduler` `scheduler/scheduler_sysbatch.go:53` · **Call site:** `nomad/worker.go:628` · **Chain:** → `nomad/worker.go:93` → `s`

---

## Route 8 — `deploymentWatcherRaftShim.apply` **(closure — no server behind it)**

- **Field:** `nomad/deployment_watcher_shims.go:15` — `apply raftApplyFn`
- **Type:** `nomad/rpc.go:806` — `type raftApplyFn func(t structs.MessageType, msg interface{}) (interface{}, uint64, error)`. **A func type, not an interface.**
- **Implementer:** `(*Server).raftApply` `nomad/rpc.go:811`
- **Assignment:** `nomad/server.go:1161` — `raftShim := &deploymentWatcherRaftShim{apply: s.raftApply}`
- **Chain:** `apply` is a **bound method value**. The Go runtime allocates a closure capturing `s` as the receiver. There is **no `*Server` field anywhere on this path** — not on the shim, not on the func. The server is reachable only from inside the closure's hidden receiver slot, which no code can read or write.
- **Consequence:** this is the one route that cannot be re-pointed by adding a setter. Fixing it requires changing the shim to hold `s *Server` (as `drainerShim` does) or holding an indirection cell.

---

## Route 9 — `Watcher.raft` (deployments)

- **Field:** `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:76` — `raft DeploymentRaftEndpoints`
- **Interface:** `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:40-68` — 5 methods: `UpsertJob`, `UpdateDeploymentStatus`, `UpdateDeploymentPromotion`, `UpdateDeploymentAllocHealth`, `UpdateAllocDesiredTransition`
- **Implementer:** `*deploymentWatcherRaftShim` — methods at `nomad/deployment_watcher_shims.go:30`, `:40`, `:45`, `:50`, `:55`, each calling `d.apply(...)` and funnelling through `convertApplyErrors` `nomad/deployment_watcher_shims.go:22`
- **Assignment:** parameter `raft` at `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:105` → stored `:112`
- **Call site:** `nomad/server.go:1166` (2nd arg of `NewDeploymentsWatcher`)
- **Chain:** `raft → *deploymentWatcherRaftShim → .apply (closure) → s`. **Terminates in Route 8's frozen closure.**

## Route 10 — `AllocUpdateBatcher.raft`

- **Field:** `nomad/deploymentwatcher/batcher.go:20` — `raft DeploymentRaftEndpoints`
- **Interface / implementer:** same as Route 9
- **Assignment:** parameter at `nomad/deploymentwatcher/batcher.go:32` (`NewAllocUpdateBatcher(ctx, batchDuration, raft DeploymentRaftEndpoints)`)
- **Call site:** `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:159` — `w.allocUpdateBatcher = NewAllocUpdateBatcher(w.ctx, w.updateBatchDuration, w.raft)`, inside `SetEnabled`
- **Chain:** `raft → (same shim as Route 9) → closure → s`
- **Lifecycle note:** created on leadership gain and set to `nil` on loss (`deployments_watcher.go:161`) — so this handle is *already* re-created per leadership epoch, but always from the same frozen `w.raft`.

## Route 11 — `Watcher.deploymentRPC` / Route 12 — `Watcher.jobRPC`

- **Fields:** `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:82` (`deploymentRPC DeploymentRPC`), `:85` (`jobRPC JobRPC`)
- **Interfaces:** `nomad/deploymentwatcher/multiregion_ce.go:13` — `type DeploymentRPC interface{}`; `:14` — `type JobRPC interface{}`. **Empty in CE**, populated in Enterprise.
- **Implementers:** the `Deployment` endpoint (`nomad/deployment_endpoint.go:22`, field `srv` at `:23`) and the `Job` endpoint (`nomad/job_endpoint.go:50`, field `srv` at `:51`)
- **Assignment:** params at `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:105` → stored `:113-114`
- **Call site:** `nomad/server.go:1166-1167` — `NewDeploymentEndpoint(s, nil)`, `NewJobEndpoints(s, nil)`
- **Chain:** `deploymentRPC → *Deployment → nomad/deployment_endpoint.go:23 → s`; `jobRPC → *Job → nomad/job_endpoint.go:51 → s`
- **Sharpest edge in the audit:** in CE the static type is `interface{}`, so the compiler enforces nothing and grep for the interface name finds two one-line declarations. These fields hold two full RPC endpoints — and via `Job`, all ten admission hooks of Route 3 — behind a type that is literally empty.

## Route 13 — `deploymentWatcher.deploymentTriggers` (embedded)

- **Field:** `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployment_watcher.go:65` — embedded `deploymentTriggers` (anonymous field, so the methods promote onto `deploymentWatcher`)
- **Interface:** `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployment_watcher.go:37-54` — 5 methods: `createUpdate`, `upsertJob`, `upsertDeploymentStatusUpdate`, `upsertDeploymentPromotion`, `upsertDeploymentAllocHealth` (unexported — package-private contract)
- **Implementer:** the parent `*Watcher` — `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:449`, `:458`, `:464`, `:476`, `:482`
- **Assignment:** parameter `triggers` at `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployment_watcher.go:114` → stored `:125`
- **Call site:** `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployments_watcher.go:303-304` — `newDeploymentWatcher(w.ctx, w.queryLimiter, w.logger, w.state, d, job, w, w.deploymentRPC, w.jobRPC)` — the `Watcher` passes **itself** as `triggers`
- **Chain:** `deploymentTriggers → *Watcher → .raft (Route 9) → shim → closure → s`. **Four hops, one of them a closure, and the field is anonymous so it has no name to grep for.** One instance per active deployment.

## Route 14 — `deploymentWatcher.DeploymentRPC` / `.JobRPC` (embedded)

- **Fields:** `nomad/deploymentwatcher/deployment_watcher.go:69` and `:73`, both anonymous
- **Assignment:** `:125-126`, from `w.deploymentRPC` / `w.jobRPC` at the call site `deployments_watcher.go:304`
- **Chain:** same as Routes 11-12, re-retained per deployment.

---

## Route 15 — `NodeDrainer.raft`

- **Field:** `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:159` — `raft RaftApplier`
- **Interface:** `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:48-51` — 2 methods: `AllocUpdateDesiredTransition`, `NodesDrainComplete`
- **Implementer:** `drainerShim` (value type, not pointer) — `nomad/drainer_shims.go:37` and `:18`
- **Carrier:** `NodeDrainerConfig.Raft` `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:116`
- **Assignment:** `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:177` — `raft: c.Raft`, inside `NewNodeDrainer` `:175`
- **Call site:** `nomad/server.go:1189` builds `shim := drainerShim{s}`; `:1191` sets `Raft: shim`; `:1200` calls `drainer.NewNodeDrainer(c)`
- **Chain:** `raft → drainerShim → nomad/drainer_shims.go:15 (s *Server) → s`
- **Contrast with Route 8:** this shim keeps a real `*Server` field and dereferences it at call time — `d.s.config.Region` `nomad/drainer_shims.go:22` and `:41`, `d.s.raftApply` `:33` and `:43`. Same "narrow to 2 methods" idiom as the deployment shim, opposite reworkability.

## Route 16 — `nodeDrainWatcher.tracker`

- **Field:** `nomad/drainer/watch_nodes.go:130` — `tracker NodeTracker`
- **Interface:** `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:55-66` — 3 methods: `TrackedNodes`, `Remove`, `Update`
- **Implementer:** `*NodeDrainer` (same package)
- **Assignment:** parameter at `nomad/drainer/watch_nodes.go:134` (`NewNodeDrainWatcher(ctx, limiter, state, logger, tracker NodeTracker)`)
- **Call site:** `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:220` — `n.nodeWatcher = n.nodeFactory(n.ctx, n.queryLimiter, n.state, n.logger, n)` — the drainer passes **itself**; factory indirection via `DrainingNodeWatcherFactory` `nomad/drainer/drainer.go:72`, concrete factory `GetNodeWatcherFactory` `:90` returning `NewNodeDrainWatcher` at `:90`, wired at `nomad/server.go:1193`
- **Chain:** `tracker → *NodeDrainer → .raft (Route 15) → drainerShim → nomad/drainer_shims.go:15 → s`

---

## Route 17 — `volumewatcher.Watcher.rpc`

- **Field:** `nomad/volumewatcher/volumes_watcher.go:26` — `rpc CSIVolumeRPC`
- **Interface:** `nomad/volumewatcher/interfaces.go:14-16` — **one method**: `Unpublish(*structs.CSIVolumeUnpublishRequest, *structs.CSIVolumeUnpublishResponse) error`. Doc comment `interfaces.go:10-13` states the purpose: "a minimal interface of the Server… to avoid circular references between the nomad package and the volumewatcher."
- **Implementer:** the `CSIVolume` endpoint — `nomad/csi_endpoint.go:29`, field `srv` at `:30`
- **Assignment:** parameter at `nomad/volumewatcher/volumes_watcher.go:52` → stored `:60`
- **Call site:** `nomad/server.go:1180` — `NewVolumesWatcher(s.logger, NewCSIVolumeEndpoint(s, nil), s.getLeaderAcl())`
- **Chain:** `rpc → *CSIVolume → nomad/csi_endpoint.go:30 → s`. A whole RPC endpoint behind a one-method interface.

## Route 18 — `volumeWatcher.rpc` (per-volume)

- **Field:** `nomad/volumewatcher/volume_watcher.go:30` — `rpc CSIVolumeRPC`
- **Assignment:** `nomad/volumewatcher/volume_watcher.go:62` — `rpc: parent.rpc`, in `newVolumeWatcher(parent *Watcher, vol *structs.CSIVolume)` `:57`
- **Call site:** `nomad/volumewatcher/volumes_watcher.go:192` — `watcher := newVolumeWatcher(w, v)`
- **Chain:** `rpc → (same `*CSIVolume` as Route 17) → `nomad/csi_endpoint.go:30` → `s`. One instance per watched volume.

**Companion value-copies on the same two hops:** `leaderAcl string` at `nomad/volumewatcher/volumes_watcher.go:29` (assigned `:64` from `s.getLeaderAcl()` **invoked** at `nomad/server.go:1180`) and `nomad/volumewatcher/volume_watcher.go:33` (assigned `:63` from `parent.leaderAcl`). No server behind these at all — just a string that was true once.

---

## Route 19 — `Authenticator.getState` **(closure)**

- **Field:** `nomad/auth/auth.go:52` — `getState StateGetter`
- **Type:** `nomad/auth/auth.go:29` — `type StateGetter func() *state.StateStore`
- **Implementer:** `(*Server).State` `nomad/server.go:2115`, whose body is `return s.fsm.State()`
- **Carrier:** `AuthenticatorConfig.StateFn` `nomad/auth/auth.go:68`
- **Assignment:** `nomad/auth/auth.go:82` — `getState: cfg.StateFn`, in `NewAuthenticator` `:77`
- **Call site:** `nomad/server.go:477` — `StateFn: s.State`
- **Chain:** bound method value; `s` lives in the closure's receiver slot. **Not re-pointable.** Note it resolves `s.fsm` *lazily* on each call, so it survives an FSM swap on Raft restore — but not a server swap.

## Route 20 — `Authenticator.getLeaderACL` **(closure)**

- **Field:** `nomad/auth/auth.go:53` — `getLeaderACL LeaderACLGetter`
- **Type:** `nomad/auth/auth.go:30` — `type LeaderACLGetter func() string`
- **Implementer:** `(*Server).getLeaderAcl`
- **Carrier:** `AuthenticatorConfig.GetLeaderACLFn` `nomad/auth/auth.go:70`
- **Assignment:** `nomad/auth/auth.go:83` → **Call site:** `nomad/server.go:478` — `GetLeaderACLFn: s.getLeaderAcl`
- **Chain:** bound method value. **Not re-pointable.**

## Route 21 — `Authenticator.encrypter`

- **Field:** `nomad/auth/auth.go:64` — `encrypter Encrypter`
- **Interface:** `nomad/auth/auth.go:39-41` — **one method**: `VerifyClaim(string) (*structs.IdentityClaims, error)`
- **Implementer:** `*nomad.Encrypter` — `func (e *Encrypter) VerifyClaim` `nomad/encrypter.go:368`
- **Carrier:** `AuthenticatorConfig.Encrypter` `nomad/auth/auth.go:74`
- **Assignment:** `nomad/auth/auth.go:86` → **Call site:** `nomad/server.go:482` — `Encrypter: s.encrypter`
- **Chain:** `encrypter → *nomad.Encrypter → nomad/encrypter.go:51 (srv *Server) → s`
- **Note:** the `nomad/auth` package deliberately imports nothing from `nomad` — it declares its own `RPCContext` interface too (`auth.go:32-37`) — yet through this one-method field it retains the server. Alongside it sit pure **value snapshots** that can never be refreshed: `region` `auth.go:54` (from `nomad/server.go:481`), `aclsEnabled` `:44`, and the derived `validServerCertNames`/`validClientCertNames` `:56-57` built at `:87-91`.

---

## Route 22 — `HTTPServer.agent`

- **Field:** `command/agent/http.go:102` — `agent RPCer`

## Summary: the three shapes, and what each costs to fix

| Shape | Routes | What sits between field and server | Re-pointable? |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Interface → struct with `*Server` field** | 2, 3, 4-7, 11-12, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 21, 22 | one or more structs, each dereferencing `.srv`/`.s` at call time | **Yes** — a shared indirection cell behind those fields fixes all of them at once |
| **Interface → `*Server` itself** | 1 | nothing; `s` is the interface value | **Yes**, if the interface value can be replaced |
| **Bound method value** | 8 (and 9, 10, 13 which terminate in it), 19, 20 | a closure; **no `*Server` field exists on the path** | **No** — requires changing the handover shape at `nomad/server.go:1161`, `:477`, `:478` |

The deepest single chain is Route 13: `deploymentWatcher.deploymentTriggers` (anonymous field) → `*Watcher` → `.raft` → `*deploymentWatcherRaftShim` → `.apply` closure → `s` — four hops, an unnamed field, an unexported interface, and a frozen closure at the end. It is also instantiated once per active deployment.

The widest blast radius from one assignment is Route 11/12: two fields typed `interface{}` in CE (`nomad/deploymentwatcher/multiregion_ce.go:13-14`) that between them retain two RPC endpoints and all ten job admission hooks.

| **Interface → `*Server` itself** | 1 | nothing; `s` is the interface value | **Yes**, if the interface value can be replaced |
| **Bound method value** | 8 (and 9, 10, 13 which terminate in it), 19, 20 | a closure; **no `*Server` field exists on the path** | **No** — requires changing the handover shape at `nomad/server.go:1161`, `:477`, `:478` |

The deepest single chain is Route 13: `deploymentWatcher.deploymentTriggers` (anonymous field) → `*Watcher` → `.raft` → `*deploymentWatcherRaftShim` → `.apply` closure → `s` — four hops, an unnamed field, an unexported interface, and a frozen closure at the end. It is also instantiated once per active deployment.

The widest blast radius from one assignment is Route 11/12: two fields typed `interface{}` in CE (`nomad/deploymentwatcher/multiregion_ce.go:13-14`) that between them retain two RPC endpoints and all ten job admission hooks.
